records.ety: [AS. storm; akin to D. storm, G. sturm, Icel. stormr; and perhaps to Gr. <?/ assault, onset, Skr. s<?/ to flow, to hasten, or perhaps to L. sternere to strew, prostrate (cf. Stratum). √166.]
usage: Storm, Tempest. Storm is violent agitation, a commotion of the elements by wind, etc., but not necessarily implying the fall of anything from the clouds. Hence, to call a mere fall or rain without wind a storm is a departure from the true sense of the word. A tempest is a sudden and violent storm, such as those common on the coast of Italy, where the term originated, and is usually attended by a heavy rain, with lightning and thunder.

defs: A violent disturbance of the atmosphere, attended by wind, rain, snow, hail, or thunder and lightning; hence, often, a heavy fall of rain, snow, or hail, whether accompanied with wind or not.
quotes: We hear this fearful tempest sing, Yet seek no shelter to avoid the storm.

defs: A violent agitation of human society; a civil, political, or domestic commotion; sedition, insurrection, or war; violent outbreak; clamor; tumult.
quotes: I will stir up in England some black storm.
Her sister Began to scold and raise up such a storm.

defs: A heavy shower or fall, any adverse outburst of tumultuous force; violence.
quotes: A brave man struggling in the storms of fate.

defs: A violent assault on a fortified place; a furious attempt of troops to enter and take a fortified place by scaling the walls, forcing the gates, or the like.
quotes: Storms beat, and rolls the main; O! beat those storms, and roll the seas, in vain.
What at first was called a gust, the same Hath now a storm's, anon a tempest's name.
notes: ☞ Storm is often used in the formation of self-explained compounds; as, storm-presaging, stormproof, storm-tossed, and the like.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stormed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Storming.]

defs: To assault; to attack, and attempt to take, by scaling walls, forcing gates, breaches, or the like; as, to storm a fortified town.
To blow with violence; also, to rain, hail, snow, or the like, usually in a violent manner, or with high wind; -- used impersonally; as, it storms.
To rage; to be in a violent passion; to fume.
quotes: The master storms, the lady scolds.
